mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly compress the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in real retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and residential demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly contractions in the index of industrial production reflect tightening credit conditions and falling factory output, which directly reduce corporate profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns when credit spreads exceed 300 bps, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate falling below 2.5 percent signals emergency central bank liquidity provision, which stabilizes market funding conditions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are negative in >65% of instances when the rate crosses below 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of non-farm payrolls declining or stagnating alongside a rising unemployment rate confirms a structural labor market breakdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses sign during periods of high inflation, or triggered instances show positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in chained-dollar GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 60-day equity returns in >55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price index readings and a declining federal funds rate creates stagflation expectations, which compress real discount rates and equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when oil prices are stable, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.
